description:
You are a mobile build-pipeline engineer preparing a fresh Linux CI runner.  
Create and verify a Python virtual environment using the built-in venv module.

1. Make a new virtual environment whose absolute path is exactly  
   /home/user/mobile-pipeline-env

2. After the virtual environment has been created, generate a plain-text log file named  
   /home/user/mobile-pipeline-env-setup.log

3. The log file must contain **exactly** three non-empty lines (no extra spaces before/after text, newline-terminated after each line) in the following order:

   ENV_PATH=/home/user/mobile-pipeline-env  
   PYTHON_EXECUTABLE=/home/user/mobile-pipeline-env/bin/python  
   LOG_STATUS=SUCCESS  

   • The first two lines are constant strings—do not substitute any other path.  
   • The third line must read LOG_STATUS=SUCCESS only after you have confirmed that both
     “/home/user/mobile-pipeline-env/bin/activate” **and**
     “/home/user/mobile-pipeline-env/bin/python” exist.

The automated checker will fail if:
• The directory /home/user/mobile-pipeline-env is missing.  
• Either activate or python is missing inside the bin/ subdirectory.  
• /home/user/mobile-pipeline-env-setup.log is missing, has more/less than three lines,
  has extra blank lines, or any line deviates from the strings shown above.

Carry out the steps in the terminal; do not show the exact commands here—only execute them inside the shell during the session. When finished, the log file and virtual-environment directory must satisfy the conditions above. No other output files are required.
truth:
Expected artifacts after successful completion:

1. Directory /home/user/mobile-pipeline-env/ exists.
   Required entries inside it:
   • /home/user/mobile-pipeline-env/bin/activate  (regular file, any size)
   • /home/user/mobile-pipeline-env/bin/python    (regular file, any size, executable)

2. File /home/user/mobile-pipeline-env-setup.log exists with **exact** byte contents:

ENV_PATH=/home/user/mobile-pipeline-env\n
PYTHON_EXECUTABLE=/home/user/mobile-pipeline-env/bin/python\n
LOG_STATUS=SUCCESS\n

(no additional lines, spaces, or characters).

Automated tests will:
• stat the two required files in bin/.
• cat the log file and compare it byte-for-byte to the string above.
